learning_outcomes.description: Understand the stages of quantitative research
Understand how social theories are related to social scientific concepts
Be able to calculate and interpret basic univariate descriptive statistics
Be able to construct and interpret two and three-way cross-tabulations
Understand the logic of classical statistical inference
Be able to make statistical inferences based on a sample mean/proportion or comparison of means/proportions
Be able to carry out a chi-squared test of statistical independence for tabular data
Be able to construct and interpret a correlation coefficient
Appreciate the logic of bivariate linear regression
Be able to use Jamovi statistical software to access and manipulate survey data in preparation for quantitative analysis
Be able to use Jamovi statistical software to carry out simple descriptive and inferential statistical procedures.

assessments.due_date:
In-semester Saturday

5/09/2026 - 20/09/2026

This exam will be scheduled by UQ central examinations and will take place during the Semester 2 saturday examination period.
assessments.task_description:
This is a multi-choice assessing students' knowledge of statistical concepts learnt in the lectures and tutorials. The questions will assess both knowledge and capacity to interpret statistical information.

Marking criteria: Criteria including rubrics are available in the ‘Assessments’ folder on the course Blackboard site.

AI Use: This assessment task will be completed in-person. The use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct Policy.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.category: Computer Code, Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Tutorial/ Problem Set
assessments.task_description:
This assessment item consists of a mix of statistics/data analysis problems that must be completed using Jamovi software for analysis. The computer exercises will be posted to Blackboard in Week 9.

You must upload your assignment as one file that contains three parts to a Blackboard link. Please use the word document that is provided to structure your assignment. You can type the hand calculations or insert images (photos) of your hand written text. Part A includes a section on hand calculations. Part B includes a section on Jamovi software based exercises. Part C is a statement of transferable skills. 

Part A focuses on manual calculations (3 questions total)

Part B focuses on Jamovi calculations (7 questions total)

Part C focuses on real world applications and definitions (3 questions total)

Marking criteria: Criteria including rubrics are available in the ‘Assessments’ folder on the course Blackboard site.

AI Use: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which AI may provide limited support and guidance. A failure to reference AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct Policy. Students may be required to complete an AI usage declaration and submit it with their assessment. Additional resources are available through UQ AI Student Hub.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assessment item must be submitted via Turnitin on Blackboard Ultra.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Quiz 1 (week 3) 10/08/2026 - 12/08/2026

Quiz 2 (week 6) 31/08/2026 - 2/09/2026

Quiz 3 (week 11) 12/10/2026 - 14/10/2026

Each review quiz will be due 48 hours after it is assigned.

Quiz 1 (week 3) will be open Monday 10th August 2pm, due date 12th August 2pm.

Quiz 2 (week 6) will be open Monday 31st August 2pm, due date 2nd September 2pm.

Quiz 3 (week 11) will be open Monday 12th October 2pm, due date 14th October 2pm.
assessments.task_description:
In weeks 3, 6 and 11 there will be review tests that are designed to help you a) practice for the mid-semester exam, and b) make progress on your assignments. There are 3 tests to complete over the semester.

The tests can be found on the course site under the Assessment tab. They are to be submitted online within 48 hours from when the date and time the test opens. Tests are set up as online quizzes. Solutions will be made available on the blackboard site after the due date, and no late submissions will be accepted without prior approval from the course coordinator. If you are unable to complete the tests within the given period you will need to apply for a deferred exam no later than 5 calendar days after the due date. 

Tests are not graded by the course coordinator or the tutors - they are graded automatically through blackboard. It is your responsibility to look at the answers provided. Tests are graded out of a total of questions across the semester answered correctly. These must be completed by the due dates. Each week, the number of questions will vary - some weeks might have 10 questions, others might have only 4 questions. This reflects the time needed to complete each set of questions.

The tests will be available in the Assessment folder on blackboard. Once you start the test you will be required to complete it in one sitting. You will not be able to pause the test or come back at a later time. The test must be completed within a maximum of one hour. After one hour has passed the test will automatically close and submit the responses that you have saved.

Further details about tests will be provided in week 1 of the semester.

Marking criteria: Criteria including rubrics are available in the ‘Assessments’ folder on the course Blackboard site.

AI Use: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which AI may provide limited support and guidance. A failure to reference AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct Policy. Students may be required to complete an AI usage declaration and submit it with their assessment. Additional resources are available through UQ AI Student Hub.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The tests will be available in the Assessment folder on blackboard. Once you begin the test you will need to complete it in one sitting. You will not be able to pause or return to the test later. You will have a maximum of one hour to complete the test. After 60 minutes has passed the test will automatically close and submit the responses that you have saved.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
